definitions: The practice of rigorously challenging plans, policies, systems, or assumptions by adopting an adversarial mindset.
A simulated cyberattack or physical security breach conducted by an independent group to test an organization's defenses.
examples: Red-teaming revealed critical vulnerabilities in the network.
The board requested a round of red-teaming for the new strategy.
Effective red-teaming requires an unbiased external perspective.
